$schema: Skeleton Strip — hybrid concreteness analysis tool for detecting interpretive prose drift
description: Build a Python CLI tool that mechanically decomposes prose into concrete vs abstract content, making invisible AI writing patterns visible to LLM agents. Two analysis layers: Brysbaert concreteness norms (word-level scoring) and regex pattern matching (structural interpretive filter detection). Integrates into the Ravencrest prose pipeline as an 'analytic hint' for the gate agent and a self-check for the writer agent.
assumptions: pip install is available for adding pandas
Internet access available for downloading Brysbaert norms from HuggingFace (one-time)
Gate agent and writer agent are dispatched via the ravencrest-pusher-orchestrator
The gate agent can invoke bash commands (tools: ['*'] in frontmatter)
The writer agent can invoke bash commands (tools: ['*'] in frontmatter)

designDecisions.rationale: We're doing dictionary lookup, not dependency parsing. Fewer dependencies, faster execution, no model downloads. The Brysbaert norms contain many inflected forms already.

designDecisions.decision: Two analysis layers (vocabulary + structure) over a single model-based approach
designDecisions.rationale: Vocabulary scoring catches abstract word choice. Pattern matching catches interpretive structures that can use concrete words. Neither alone is sufficient. Combined, they cover both failure modes. Both are deterministic — no LLM bias in the analysis layer.

designDecisions.decision: Gate reads JSON output, not skeleton view
designDecisions.rationale: The JSON format provides structured, parseable data the LLM can reference precisely. The skeleton view is for human debugging. The gate needs paragraph indices, density numbers, and pattern names — not visual formatting.

designDecisions.decision: Writer uses summary mode, not full JSON
designDecisions.rationale: The writer's self-check should be lightweight — 5-10 lines of output, not a full report. The writer rewrites flagged zones; it doesn't need per-sentence breakdown. Summary mode keeps context burn minimal.

designDecisions.decision: Graceful degradation if tool is unavailable
designDecisions.rationale: The skeleton strip is an enhancement, not a gate. If pip install fails, if the norms aren't downloaded, if the script has a bug — the pipeline must continue. The gate falls back to blind Check 8 (which existed before this tool). Never let a tooling failure block prose delivery.

tasks.title: Create requirements.txt and install dependencies
tasks.description: Create the tools/skeleton-strip/ directory structure and requirements.txt with pandas dependency. Install via pip.
acceptanceCriteria: tools/skeleton-strip/ directory exists with __init__.py
requirements.txt lists pandas>=2.0
pip install -r requirements.txt succeeds
python3 -c 'import pandas' succeeds

tasks.title: Download and cache Brysbaert concreteness norms
tasks.description: Create download script that fetches the Brysbaert CSV from HuggingFace and caches it locally. Idempotent — skips if already present.
acceptanceCriteria: Running the script downloads CSV to data/concreteness_norms.csv
CSV has columns Word and Conc.M
CSV has ≥39,000 rows
Running again prints 'Already downloaded' and exits cleanly

tasks.title: Build word-level scoring module
tasks.description: Core scoring engine: loads norms, tokenizes text, scores words, classifies as concrete/abstract. Returns SentenceScore dataclass.
acceptanceCriteria: load_norms() builds dict from CSV with ~40K entries
tokenize('She picked up her coffee.') returns ['she', 'picked', 'up', 'her', 'coffee']
score_word('coffee', norms) returns ~4.9
score_word('deliberation', norms) returns ~1.8
score_word('the', norms) returns None
score_sentence() returns SentenceScore with concrete_words, abstract_words, concrete_ratio

tasks.title: Build interpretive pattern matching module
tasks.description: Regex-based pattern matcher for the 7 core interpretive filter flags plus extensions. Returns PatternMatch objects with positions.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines ≥10 regex patterns covering 7 core flags plus extensions
find_patterns on 'kind of deliberation that suggested' returns ≥1 match
find_patterns on 'She picked up her coffee' returns 0 matches
find_patterns on 'Something about the way she walked betrayed' returns ≥2 matches
Matches include character offsets
Case-insensitive matching works

tasks.title: Build sentence splitter
tasks.description: Prose-aware sentence and paragraph splitting that handles abbreviations, dialogue, ellipses, and em-dashes.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Dr. Mars walked in.' is one sentence
Dialogue with internal punctuation is one sentence
Ellipses followed by lowercase don't split
Paragraphs split on double newlines
Each Sentence has index, paragraph_index, start/end char offsets

tasks.title: Build the strip analysis engine
tasks.description: Combines scorer and patterns into SentenceAnalysis, ParagraphAnalysis, and DocumentAnalysis. Produces skeleton strings and verdicts.
acceptanceCriteria: analyze_sentence produces correct verdict for known good/bad examples
Skeleton of 'Chin up. Steps even.' retains most content
Skeleton of 'kind of deliberation that suggested' is nearly empty
analyze_paragraph correctly flags abstract zones
analyze_document aggregates paragraph analyses with overall verdict

tasks.title: Build the CLI entry point
tasks.description: Main CLI tool with three output modes: --json (gate consumption), --skeleton (human debugging), --summary (writer self-check).
acceptanceCriteria: --json produces valid JSON to stdout
--skeleton produces readable skeleton view with density indicators
--summary produces concise flagged-zone output
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on file not found, 2 on missing norms
Handles markdown with YAML frontmatter (skips it)

tasks.title: Add skeleton strip invocation to gate agent Check 8
tasks.description: Gate runs the skeleton strip CLI before Check 8, reads JSON output as 'analytic hint', focuses Check 8 on flagged zones. Graceful degradation if tool unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: Gate instructions include bash invocation of strip.py --json
Gate reads JSON output before running Check 8
Gate report includes skeleton_data in interpretive_filter check
Gate focuses on flagged zones first, then scans remainder
If strip.py fails, gate falls back to blind Check 8 with logged warning

tasks.title: Add skeleton strip pre-flight to orchestrator
tasks.description: Orchestrator checks if skeleton strip tool is available before prose pipeline. Passes availability flag to gate dispatch. Pipeline continues even if unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator runs python3 strip.py --help as pre-flight check
Gate dispatch prompt includes tool availability flag
Pipeline continues when tool is unavailable (degraded mode)

tasks.title: Add self-check instructions to writer agent
tasks.description: Writer runs strip.py --summary after drafting. Rewrites flagged zones in place before delivery. Tool failure does not block delivery.
acceptanceCriteria: Writer instructions include skeleton strip self-check section
Self-check uses --summary mode (lightweight output)
Writer rewrites flagged zones before delivery
Tool failure does not block delivery (graceful degradation)
Delivery flow documented: write → strip check → fix → deliver

tasks.title: Build test fixtures from known good/bad prose
tasks.description: Collect known-good and known-bad sentences from voice samples and 18-interpretive-filter.md into test fixtures. Include edge cases.
acceptanceCriteria: ≥20 known-good sentences from voice samples and reference file
≥20 known-bad sentences from reference file
≥5 edge cases with expected verdicts (interior monologue, dialogue, deliberate metaphor)

tasks.title: Run calibration and adjust thresholds
tasks.description: Run full pipeline against all fixtures. Report accuracy. Adjust thresholds if needed. Target: ≥80% true positive, ≥85% true negative.
acceptanceCriteria: True positive rate ≥80% (bad sentences correctly flagged)
True negative rate ≥85% (good sentences correctly passed)
False positive rate ≤15%
Edge cases with 'should_pass' pass ≥80%
Calibration report printed with threshold recommendations

tasks.title: Run end-to-end test against voice samples
tasks.description: Run strip.py --json against the full voice-samples.md file. Verify overall verdict is grounded/mixed, zero abstract paragraphs.
acceptanceCriteria: Voice samples produce overall verdict 'grounded' or 'mixed'
Zero paragraphs flagged as 'abstract'
Any unexpected flags investigated and documented

tasks.title: Write README with usage, thresholds, and limitations
tasks.description: Document the tool: theory, installation, usage, thresholds, calibration results, known limitations.
acceptanceCriteria: README has installation instructions
Three CLI modes documented with examples
Thresholds documented with rationale
Known limitations listed (function words, proper nouns, interior monologue)
Calibration accuracy numbers included

steps.note: Create directory structure and install pandas
Build all three independent modules in parallel: scorer, patterns, splitter
Build strip engine (depends on scorer + patterns + splitter)
Build CLI entry point (depends on engine)
Gate integration and writer integration (both depend on CLI, independent of each other)
Build test fixtures from reference files
End-to-end voice sample test and README (independent)
